title: Global disruptions affect the aluminium market: March 2026 update
summary: The aluminium market has been severely disrupted by geopolitical tensions and logistical problems. This update covers impacts on production and capacity amid ongoing global challenges. Market participants are monitoring supply chain issues closely.

title: FACTBOX: Aluminum prices surge as Middle East war disrupts GCC trade flows
summary: The war in the Middle East has disrupted aluminum production and exports from GCC countries, which produced 6.16 million metric tons in 2025, about 8% of global output. Key smelters like Emirates Global Aluminium, Alba, and others face challenges. US Midwest premiums and Asian spot premiums have surged due to supply concerns.

title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs amid fears of prolonged supply disruptions from Middle East conflicts, including shutdowns at Alba representing 19% of its capacity and force majeure declarations. The region supplies about 9% of global aluminum. EGA reroutes exports and maintains stocks to mitigate impacts.

title: Century Aluminum amps up American aluminum production
summary: Century Aluminum is boosting U.S. production in South Carolina, supported by Trump-era tariffs. The report highlights efforts to increase domestic aluminum output amid global market dynamics.

title: Commodity Update – March 2026
summary: Aluminum prices surged due to Middle East conflicts disrupting 9% of global production, with LME prices rising and U.S. Midwest premiums hitting records. Emirates Global Aluminum and Century Aluminum announced a new facility in Oklahoma to boost domestic capacity. Inventory withdrawals have doubled amid supply chain risks.

title: Aluminium Industry Updates: Recycling, Innovation & Investment
summary: Henkel Adhesive Technologies showcases sustainable metal packaging innovations at METPACK 2026 to boost recycling and reduce emissions. Constellium’s 2025 Sustainability Report highlights a 14% drop in Scope 1 and 2 emissions intensity and 47% recycled aluminium input, exceeding 2026 targets. Rio Tinto and Prysmian trial low-carbon aluminium cables using ELYSIS technology for data centers, eliminating direct GHG emissions.

title: Aluminum Extrusion Market Set to Hit USD 202.23 Billion by 2035, Driven by Rising Demand for Lightweight and Sustainable Materials
summary: The aluminum extrusion market is projected to reach USD 202.23 billion by 2035 due to demand for lightweight, durable, corrosion-resistant materials. Growth is fueled by needs for complex shapes, higher strength, design flexibility, energy efficiency, and sustainable materials in various sectors.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 introduces Innovation Awards celebrating breakthroughs in sustainable manufacturing, technology, and smart automation. Categories include Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing, open to recent implemented solutions, pilots, prototypes, or market-validated innovations.

title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
summary: The global aluminum scrap market surges due to policy shifts, high prices, and a push toward circular economy principles with investments in recycling and green smelting. Recycled aluminum uses 95% less energy than primary production, aiding sustainability in construction, automotive, and electronics sectors, especially in Asia-Pacific.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026: Pioneering Sustainability and Innovation
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 trade fair in Düsseldorf, Germany, from October 6–8 focuses on sustainability, innovation, and secondary aluminum's role in decarbonization. It highlights advancements in recycling, casting, automation, and surface finishing for applications in mobility, construction, and manufacturing.

title: Aluminium Industry in 2026: Global Trends, Innovations, and What Buyers Should Expect
summary: By 2026, aluminium drives manufacturing, clean energy, and mobility with its recyclability and properties fitting ESG frameworks. Buyers demand traceability, responsible sourcing, and energy-efficient production as sustainability becomes a procurement requirement.

title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry
summary: IBAAS 2026 in India focuses on bauxite innovation, recycling, green aluminium, AI, and sustainable practices. Key themes include red mud management, advanced alloys, cathode technologies, value recovery from wastes, and Industry 4.0 digitalization.

title: The Aluminum Renaissance: A Deep-Dive into Alcoa's (AA) 2026 Strategic Pivot
summary: Alcoa's 2026 strategy features low-carbon Sustana products like EcoSource alumina and EcoLum aluminum with under 4 kg CO2e/kg. ELYSIS technology with Rio Tinto advances to commercial validation, emitting oxygen instead of CO2, positioning Alcoa for green premiums in EV, solar, and AI data centers.

title: Industrial Metals Hit Multi-Year Highs: Aluminum and Steel Surge Amid Global Supply Constraints
summary: Hot-rolled coil (HRC) steel prices have surpassed $1,000 per ton for the first time since 2024, driven by supply deficits, AI infrastructure demand, and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Nucor raised its spot price to $1,025 per ton, while West Coast prices reached $1,075 per ton. The rally reflects China's production limits and a new era of pricing discipline in steel markets.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,126 CNY/T on March 27, 2026, up 0.13% from the previous day, amid concerns over output curbs in China's Tangshan due to air pollution responses. Chinese steel production declined 3.6% in February, contributing to global output falling 2.2%. Higher raw material costs from ocean freight and energy prices linked to the Iran war are squeezing mill margins.

title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 30.03.2026
summary: Hadeed signals upward pressure on Saudi steel pricing for February 2026 amid ongoing market dynamics. Stainless steel futures show flat pricing on 1-, 2-, and 3-month terms as of March 27, 2026. Broader news covers geopolitical impacts like Hormuz closures and attacks on Iranian steel facilities, alongside export awards.

title: Long Product Price Hikes Persist
summary: US scrap prices rose, with prime busheling Midwest scrap up $30/gt to $445-455/gt for February delivery, and shredded scrap gaining $30/gt to $445-450/gt. March expectations remain sideways to higher for various grades. Long product prices continue upward momentum with $30/ton increases for wire rods and $60/ton jumps reported.

title: Commodity Price Watch: March 2026
summary: Steel prices are higher in the US and Europe due to shipping disruptions from war and elevated electricity costs, though weak demand limits full pass-through. US mills maintain pricing power against restricted imports, while European EAF steelmaking faces exposure to rising energy prices. Asia sees little change amid these global pressures.

title: Steel Imports Up 0.8% In February vs. January
summary: U.S. steel imports totaled 1,663,000 net tons in February 2026, up 0.8% from January, while finished steel imports were 1,171,000 net tons, down 6.2%. Year-to-date imports are down 37.6% for total and 38.5% for finished steel compared to 2025. Finished steel import market share was estimated at 15% for February and the first two months of 2026.

title: US raw steel production increases by 0.4 percent - week 12, 2026
summary: US raw steel production for the week ending March 21, 2026, reached 1.781 million net tons at a 77.0% capacity utilization rate, up 0.4% from the prior week. This represents a 4.6% increase from the same week in 2025. Year-to-date production through March 21 is up 4.9% at 77.2% capacity utilization.

title: U.S. steel imports rose 0.8% on a monthly basis in February
summary: U.S. total steel imports rose 0.8% to 1,663,000 net tons in February 2026 from January, while finished steel imports fell 6.2% to 1,171,000 net tons. Year-to-date declines are 37.6% for total and 38.5% for finished steel versus 2025. Over the 12 months to February 2026, imports dropped 20.3% total and 24.7% for finished products.

title: TA Launches Steel Transportation and Sustainability Index, Revealing 30% Decline in U.S. Steel Imports
summary: TA Services launched an inaugural Steel Transportation and Sustainability Index showing U.S. steel imports declined 30% from a January 2025 peak of 2.8 million metric tons to 1.4-1.6 million metric tons by early 2026. The report highlights accelerating sustainability expectations across the steel supply chain, including increased demand for emissions transparency and Scope 3 disclosures driven by regulations like the EU's Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive.

title: Ethisphere Names U.S. Steel One of the World's Most Ethical Companies® for Fifth Consecutive Year
summary: United States Steel Corporation was recognized as one of the World's Most Ethical Companies for the fifth consecutive year by Ethisphere. U.S. Steel is the only honoree in the Metals, Minerals, & Mining category, demonstrating its leadership in advancing business ethics.

title: Worthington Steel Declares Quarterly Dividend
summary: Worthington Steel announced a quarterly dividend declaration as part of routine corporate announcements in the steel sector.

title: Copper prices drop amid global growth fears, plump stocks
summary: Copper prices on the London Metal Exchange fell 9.4% to $12,081.74 per metric ton as of March 23, driven by fears of weakened economic growth from higher energy prices amid the US-Israel war with Iran. Global copper inventories have surpassed 1 million metric tons for the first time since 2003, increasing supply availability. Analysts note the sell-off aligns with broader market concerns and rising energy costs impacting industrial metal demand.

title: Copper Scrap Prices Jump $0.09 | March 25, 2026
summary: Copper scrap prices increased by $0.09 per pound on March 25, 2026, leading the market higher according to the ScrapMonster Price Index. Key grades saw this uplift amid broader scrap market movements.

title: Most of copper's rally has happened, Goldman Sachs says. They fear a correction is coming
summary: Goldman Sachs forecasts a growing copper surplus in 2026, raising their estimate to 300,000 metric tons due to cooled demand and higher scrap supply from record prices. They predict an 18% drop in copper prices to $11,000 per ton by December 2026, after a 23% rally since November driven by speculation. Global inventories are building ahead of Lunar New Year restocking, with downside risks to demand growth.

title: Copper price soars to 7-month high on China's plans to cut output
summary: Copper prices reached their highest level in seven months following announcements from Chinese smelters, which process half of the world's mined copper, to cut output. This development drove a significant surge in copper prices amid global supply dynamics.

title: Copper, gold set to be 'big winners' in 2026
summary: White & Case's Mining & Metals 2026 report predicts copper and gold as major winners in 2026 after strong 2025 performance, with copper hitting $12,000 per tonne in December due to output reductions at major mines like Escondida, Collahuasi, and Grasberg. Copper prices have risen 6.65% in the past month and 36.08% year-over-year, currently above $5.8 per pound amid a weakening US dollar.

title: [SMM Nickel Midday Commentary] On March 30, nickel prices dropped slightly, and US voters’ dissatisfaction with Trump hit a record high.
summary: On March 30, nickel prices experienced a slight drop according to SMM's midday commentary. The report notes this movement amid broader market observations. US voters' dissatisfaction with Trump reached a record high, though its direct link to nickel pricing remains unclear.

title: TIME Recognizes ZincFive Among America's Top GreenTech Companies 2026
summary: ZincFive has been named to TIME’s America’s Top GreenTech Companies 2026 list for the third consecutive year. The recognition highlights its nickel-zinc battery solutions for immediate power applications, emphasizing safety, sustainability, and performance in demanding environments like data centers. The award is presented by TIME Magazine and Statista Inc.

title: ZincFive named to TIME green tech list for third year
summary: ZincFive ranks 142 on TIME's America's Top GreenTech Companies 2026 list for the third year running. The company specializes in nickel-zinc batteries for immediate power in data centers and UPS systems, offering high power density, safety, and sustainability as an alternative to lead-acid and lithium-ion batteries. Its patented electrochemistry avoids environmental and safety issues of conventional options.
